label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, the last day of February is the 28th, unless it's a leap year. But 2001 isn't a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, 2004 would be the next one. So her birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16-year-old birthday, which means today's date is February 28, 2017. Because 2001 + 16 is 2017. So today is 02/28/2017. Now, the question asks: What is the date a month ago in MM/DD/YYYY? So a month before today, which is 02/28/2017. So subtracting one month. That would be January 28, 2017. Because if you go back one month from February 28, you go to January 28. But wait, some people might think about it differently. For example, if you take a month off February 28, you get January 28. Because February 28 minus a month is January 28. Also, since February doesn't have 30 days, if someone said "a month ago from March 30", you would go to February 28 (or 29 if it's a leap year). But in this case, since we are going back from February 28, subtracting a month would take us to January 28. So the date a month ago is 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, that's option D. Let me check the options again.
